title: Ruagea yungasana
intro: Rauvolfia yungasana, also known as Ruagea yungasana, is a plant species native to the Andean region, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including the treatment of anxiety, insomnia, and hypertension, as it exhibits vasodilatory and sedative effects. The bioactive constituents of Ruagea yungasana include alkaloids such as yungasamine, yungasin, and rauvolsin, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make a tea or infusion, and it can also be found in herbal preparations such as extracts and tinctures.
